subject: Re: Culture transformation — working session prep
body: Daniel — reviewed the Monday.com cuts. The nursing-unit trust dip is the right place to aim. I'll build the October 16 session so the frontline managers leave with one practice they can run the next morning, not a binder. Copying Priya to lock logistics.\n\nJordan

subject: The Safety Equation — revised comps and sample chapter
body: Rick — attached the tightened comp list and the measurement chapter. I cut two soft titles and added two that sit closer to the business-driver framing. Sample chapter is the strongest of the set. Ready when you are to take it out in the spring.\n\nJordan

subject: Re: October logistics — holding the room
body: Karen — October 9 works on my end. Plan for the four desk heads plus you and Rebecca. I'll send the room a lapel mic and confidence-monitor request through Priya so Learning Events can finalize. One practice, run the next morning, same format as Session 1.\n\nJordan

subject: Re: Hogan debrief scheduling
body: Julia — the individual debriefs are ready. I'd rather run them in 45-minute blocks than pack them tighter; the value is in the conversation, not the report. Priya has my mornings Monday through Thursday. Send me the leaders' availability and she'll slot them.\n\nJordan

subject: Coaching cadence for the three VPs
body: Elaine — two of the three are settling into the work nicely. The third needs a slower runway, which is normal at this stage. Suggest we hold the biweekly cadence through the quarter and reassess in October rather than pushing. Happy to talk it through by phone.\n\nJordan

subject: Batch — this week's logistics
body: Priya — three things. Book the Crestline working-session travel for October 16, one night, close to their office. Send the Meridian Sessions 1–2 invoice reminder if it's still open at net 30. And hold my Friday 2pm writing block; no calls on top of it.\n\nJordan

subject: Re: Spring submission window
body: Rick — the October 23 follow-up works. I'll have the full proposal polished by then, including the measurement appendix editors keep asking about. If the comps still feel soft to you, flag it before we go out; I'd rather fix it now than in revisions.\n\nJordan

subject: Re: Board readout after October 16
body: Daniel — happy to draft the one-page board readout once the working session lands. I'll keep it to the trust movement on the nursing units and the two practices we're piloting. No jargon, nothing your board will need translated.\n\nJordan

subject: Batch — invoicing and CCE
body: Priya — send the Vantage coaching invoice for the quarter, and pull my CCE-hours tally so I can see how many I still owe before the December renewal. Last I checked I was at 22 of 40. Let's not leave it to the last week again.\n\nJordan

subject: Re: Next month's session
body: Patricia — good on the usual time. I want to spend this one on how I'm pacing the book against the client load. It's starting to feel like both are asking for the same Friday afternoon and only one can win. See you then.\n\nJordan

subject: Session 3 pre-read
body: Karen — attaching the team-dynamics pre-read for October. Same rule as before: ten minutes, not homework. It primes the desk heads for the exercise without giving away where it lands.\n\nJordan

subject: Re: Safety measurement dataset
body: Brian — the anonymized desk-level data is cleaner than I expected. If the trend holds it's a genuine chapter, not a sidebar. Can we grab thirty minutes next week to look at it together before I write it up? Your read always sharpens mine.\n\nJordan

subject: Re: Q4 coaching renewal
body: Elaine — glad the work is landing. Let's talk renewal after the October check-in so we're pricing against results, not hopes. Priya can hold a call for the first week of October. Same three VPs, or are you adding a fourth?\n\nJordan

subject: Batch — travel for October
body: Priya — two trips in October. Meridian is in-town on the 9th, no travel. Crestline on the 16th needs a flight and one hotel night. Book the aisle, and nothing before 8am; I'd rather not do the workshop on four hours of sleep.\n\nJordan

subject: Re: Team assessment mid-point readout
body: Julia — the mid-point readout is drafted. The headline is that the team's blind spot is upward candor, not competence. That's good news; competence is hard to move, candor isn't. I'll walk the leaders through it live rather than emailing a deck into the void.\n\nJordan

subject: Re: Podcast as a platform note
body: Rick — fair point that four pilot episodes isn't a platform yet. I'm moving Leading Out Loud to a real weekly cadence this fall, which gives us a cleaner number by the time we're out with editors. Let's not oversell it in the proposal; let's just show the trajectory.\n\nJordan

subject: Batch — evening cleanup
body: Priya — end-of-day list. Confirm the Crestline hotel is booked, send Karen the Session 3 room request, and move my Wednesday coaching call thirty minutes later so it doesn't crowd the Vantage debrief. Nothing urgent; morning is fine.\n\nJordan

subject: Re: Monday.com board access
body: Daniel — got into the board, thanks. The frontline-manager column is where I'll live between now and October 16. I added two cards for the practices we discussed so the trail is visible to your team, not just in my head. Priya's cc'd for scheduling.\n\nJordan

subject: VP coaching — midpoint note
body: Elaine — quick note before our call. The strongest signal so far is the head of R&D running her first genuine post-mortem without assigning blame. Small thing, big tell. That's the muscle we're building. More when we talk.\n\nJordan

subject: Batch — noon queue
body: Priya — noon batch. Send the David Kim quarterly-estimate reminder, confirm the Dinner and Discourse date with the caterer, and check whether the Meridian net-30 invoice cleared. If it did, mark it and move on. If not, one polite nudge.\n\nJordan
